title: myristica motleyi
intro: Myristica motleyi, also known as the Motley's nutmeg or Papuan nutmeg, is a plant species native to the island of New Guinea, belonging to the Myristicaceae family. The plant's seeds are used in traditional medicine and are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The seeds of the Myristica motleyi plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and are believed to have antioxidant and analgesic properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a powder, and are also used in traditional remedies to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
